seoTitle: How to Verify Your Photo ID on PayPal (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to prepare, capture, and upload a clean photo ID to clear PayPal's compliance checks and lift account restrictions.
intro: Uploading a photo ID to PayPal should be simple, but bad lighting or cropping often causes automated rejections. This guide covers preparing your document, capturing a clean image, and submitting it through the Resolution Center. It's meant for anyone facing a spending cap or restriction tied to identity verification.

steps.heading: Open the Resolution Center
steps.body: Go to your account notifications or settings and click through to the Resolution Center to see your active verification request.

steps.heading: Choose your document type
steps.body: Select which acceptable ID you'll use, such as a driver's license, passport, or national ID card, from the dropdown.

steps.heading: Prepare good lighting and a flat surface
steps.body: Place your physical ID on a dark, flat surface under natural light to avoid glare that can cause a rejected scan.

steps.heading: Capture a clear high-resolution photo
steps.body: Take a photo showing all four edges of the document with readable, unblurred text.

steps.heading: Upload and confirm your name matches
steps.body: Drop the image into the secure upload box, verify your legal name matches your profile, and submit for review.

faq.question: Why does my upload keep getting rejected?
faq.answer: Rejections are usually caused by glare, blur, cropped edges, or a name mismatch between your ID and your PayPal profile.

faq.question: How long does compliance review take?
faq.answer: Review timelines vary, but most submissions are processed within a few business days once the image quality passes automated checks.

faq.question: Which documents does PayPal accept?
faq.answer: Common accepted documents include a driver's license, passport, or national identity card, depending on your country.

faq.question: Does this remove all my account restrictions?
faq.answer: Photo ID verification clears the specific identity-related hold, but other outstanding compliance items may still need to be resolved separately.
